message:
fix(supervisor): add proper HTTP client timeouts for health checks

The checkHealthHTTP function was using http.DefaultClient which has no
connection timeout, TLS handshake timeout, or idle timeout configured.
While the request context has a 3-second timeout, this doesn't protect
against connection setup hangs (DNS resolution, TCP handshake, TLS
negotiation can all hang indefinitely with DefaultClient).

Added a dedicated healthCheckClient with proper timeouts:
- Overall timeout: 5 seconds
- Dial timeout: 2 seconds
- TLS handshake timeout: 2 seconds
- Response header timeout: 3 seconds
- Idle connection timeout: 30 seconds
- Connection pool limits to prevent resource exhaustion

This is consistent with the fix made to AMAdapter.HealthCheck in ce579ab.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
